"Far General Hospital, it was called Methodist Hospital. It's now merit-wessling out on 98"; canonical Forrest General Hospital (now Merit Health Wesley) // [PASS-6: confirmed

"if there was something that you wanted to do that wasn't taught in Mississippi, you could be sitting out of the state in the state with Peyton DeVille"; the canonical "out-of-state tuition" segregation evasion — sending Black students out-of-state for graduate/professional school to avoid integrating in-state grad schools; "Peyton DeVille" is Whisper-garbled; possible candidates: "paying tuition" or a specific case-name reference // [PASS-6: rejected — speculation without corroboration
"Mrs. James had prepared us well on how on the Constitution... Mrs. Marjorie James with our world history teacher"; Rowan High School teacher; identification // [PASS-6: confirmed

"Just as soon as they started taking care, taking Medicare, can have federal money"; speaker is describing the canonical Hill-Burton hospital-integration mechanism

pending_context.notes: "Really fun. Huge guy. About like this. About six, three, four. But he had the tiny little heads. I went a huge first with the tiny little head" — Branch's physical description of Theron Lynd; "first" Whisper-mangled from "fist" or "frame"

"Mr. JC Ferreland. Mr. James Bourne. And Mr. Vernon Damer" — canonical Hattiesburg NAACP member; one of the three NAACP escorts who walked Branch to register to vote with Theron Lynd; speaker's recall; "Mr. Goon" is a speaker self-correction in the transcript
Cross-references Pass 1 #100.64 — Rowan High School world-history teacher who prepared Branch on the Mississippi Constitution; Pass 1 already identified // [PASS-6: confirmed

Cross-references Pass 1 #100.41 — canonical false-witness Johnny Lee Roberts (the Forrest County feed-store worker who falsely testified Kennard ordered the chicken feed Roberts swept up); Jerry Mitchell's 2006-onward investigation eventually got Roberts to tell Branch the true story (Roberts had been instructed by white feed-store owners) // [PASS-6: confirmed

Branch's first-person account of seeing a Hattiesburg NAACP poster from Washington DC that documented her grandfather Lee Val Graves attempting to register to vote at Theron Lynd's counter — canonical pre-Brand-Smith-1965 Hattiesburg voter-registration documentation
Branch's grandfather; canonical name spelled out in transcript

pending_context.notes: "she would go somewhere different, California, Washington state, every year following the Federated Women's Club" — canonical Black women's national organizing federation founded 1896; Branch's great-grandmother attended national conventions via her sons' Pullman-porter rail passes

pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — possibly Jacksonville, Florida OR Jacksonville, Mississippi)
pending_context.notes: Raw SRT line 51: "I stayed in Jacksonville a while, two years, then I moved back to So-So." Speaker (Smith) — born Jones County MS Jan 1, 1940 — references a two-year Jacksonville stay before returning to Soso. Ambiguous; could be Jacksonville FL (long-canonical Southern Black migration destination) or one of the small Mississippi communities named Jacksonville. Speaker-originating; treat as recollection but flag for adversarial geographic clarification.

pending_context.candidate_correction: (uncertain — possibly "Anystruzel" street name OR "and the strudel" food-shop name)
pending_context.notes: Raw SRT line 4547: "Tempton and some street over the anystruzel." Following the "Tempton" street-name, this is the second corner-coordinate of the Hattiesburg location where Jill Wakeman's host lived. Speaker may be referring to a specific Hattiesburg bakery/shop landmark, or "anystruzel" may be a Whisper-mangled second street name. Net-new flag.
